svg.text: Binary AST Wire Format - Concrete Example
import { add } from "./math"; const a = add(1, 2);

g.text: section offsets: strOff | strData | extData | structData | nodes

svg.text: Section 2: String Offsets (8 bytes/string, 5 entries = 40 bytes)
Each entry = [start, end] pair of uint32 offsets into String Data
Section 3: String Data (50 bytes = source text only)

g.text: i m p o r t { a d d } f r o m " . / m a t h " ; \n c o n s t a = a d d ( 1 , 2 ) ;

svg.text: All identifiers ("add", "a") and string literals ("./math") are substrings of this source text -> zero-copy

g.text: SourceFile (48 bytes): text, fileName, path, imports offset -> Structured Data

svg.text: SourceFile Extended Data imports offset points to this section

g.text: = node index of Node[8] (StringLiteral "./math"), the moduleSpecifier

svg.text: Section 6: Nodes (28 bytes/node x 20 nodes = 560 bytes)
Flat array in source order. Each node = 7 x uint32.

g.text: 0b00 Children: bitmask of present children in visitor order (most nodes)
0b10 Extended: offset into Extended Data section (SourceFile, StringLiteral, NumericLiteral)
NodeList (kind=0xFFFFFFFF): data field = child count. Parent adjusted by decoder.
Header: 44 + StrOff: 5x8=40 + StrData: 50 + ExtData: 72 + StructData: ~4 + Nodes: 20x28=560 = ~770 bytes
* fileName/path strings would add to string table; values shown are illustrative
|- VarStmt[9] -> VarDeclList[10](const) -> [11] -> VarDecl[12] -> Ident"a"[13], CallExpr[14] -> Ident"add"[15], [16] -> Num"1"[17], Num"2"[18]
